message:
fix(resize-scheduler): fix superseded completion semantics and enable deadlock repro test

Fix the resize scheduler's handling of completions that arrive after
a newer intent has superseded the active work:

- complete_active() now properly clears active_seq, active_phase, and
  active_phase_started_at_ms when rejecting a stale completion, so the
  pane state machine is left clean for the next intent
- Use ActiveCancelledSuperseded lifecycle detail (with by_seq) instead
  of ActiveCompletionRejected, accurately reflecting that the work was
  superseded rather than simply rejected
- Remove test_deadlock_when_completing_superseded_work #[ignore] attr
  since the deadlock has been fixed
- Remove debug_snapshot_invariants_detect_pending_active_sequence_inversion
  test that relied on manually constructing an invalid state that can
  no longer occur with the fixed completion path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
